> Generated from the `BeamPayRouter.sol` NatSpec. For deployed addresses see [addresses.md](./addresses.md); for the load-bearing security properties see [invariants.md](./invariants.md).

# BeamPayRouter — Contract Reference
**Inherits:**
EIP712

**Title:**
BeamPayRouter

BeamPay protocol main contract. Merchants get paid via pay(); merchants
issue refunds via refund(). No admin, no pause, no emergency.

Hard commitment: this contract has **no pause function, no emergency
multisig, no admin backdoor**. Once deployed, pay() is callable forever.
Every parameter change waits out the 7-day timelock.


## State Variables
### FEE_RATE_HARD_LIMIT
Fee rate ceiling = 0.1% (10 bps). `constant` — no governance op can exceed it.

The single hard commitment to merchants. v1.1+ has no per-tx fee cap;
fee scales linearly so low-ticket merchants don't see distorted effective rates.


```solidity
uint256 public constant FEE_RATE_HARD_LIMIT = 10
```


### TIMELOCK_DELAY
Timelock delay for governance parameter changes = 7 days.


```solidity
uint256 public constant TIMELOCK_DELAY = 7 days
```


### BASIS_POINTS_DENOMINATOR
Basis-point denominator (10000 => "per-myriad").


```solidity
uint256 public constant BASIS_POINTS_DENOMINATOR = 10000
```


### MAX_FEE_RECIPIENTS
Cap on size of fee-recipient pool (gas-DoS guard + governance abuse cap).


```solidity
uint256 public constant MAX_FEE_RECIPIENTS = 20
```


### NATIVE_TOKEN
Sentinel address representing the chain's native asset (ETH/BNB).

1inch/Curve convention; chosen so it cannot collide with any real ERC20.
Must still be added to the whitelist via addToken(NATIVE_TOKEN).


```solidity
address public constant NATIVE_TOKEN = 0xEeeeeEeeeEeEeeEeEeEeeEEEeeeeEeeeeeeeEEeE
```


### ORDER_TYPEHASH
EIP-712 typehash for the merchant-signed `Order` struct.

Field order MUST match the front-end's `signTypedData` payload exactly.
v1.4: `receiver` inserted immediately after `merchant`.


```solidity
bytes32 public constant ORDER_TYPEHASH = keccak256(
    "Order(address merchant,address receiver,address signer,address token,uint256 amount,bytes32 orderId,uint64 createdAt,uint64 expiresAt)"
)
```


### governance
Governance multisig (2/3 recommended).

Can only mutate parameters through the 7-day timelock.
No pause power, no arbitrary withdrawal power.


```solidity
address public governance
```


### pendingGovernance
Pending new governance address for the two-step handoff.

Audit fix M-03: prevents permanent loss of governance via single-step typo.


```solidity
address public pendingGovernance
```


### currentFeeRate
Currently effective fee rate (bps). Adjustable via 7-day timelock.


```solidity
uint256 public currentFeeRate
```


### pending

```solidity
PendingChange public pending
```


### feeRecipients
Pool of fee-receiving addresses; orderId hash modulo selects one per pay.


```solidity
address[] public feeRecipients
```


### isFeeRecipient
Membership flag for O(1) duplicate detection.

Audit fix H-04: feeRecipients had no de-duplication.


```solidity
mapping(address => bool) public isFeeRecipient
```


### allowedTokens
Whitelist of accepted tokens (incl. NATIVE_TOKEN once governance adds it).


```solidity
mapping(address => bool) public allowedTokens
```


### orders
Order key (keccak256(merchant, orderId)) -> OrderRecord.


```solidity
mapping(bytes32 => OrderRecord) public orders
```


### merchantSigner
Per-merchant authorized signer. address(0) = no delegate; only the merchant
itself may sign orders. Setting a non-zero value lets that wallet co-sign on
the merchant's behalf. Self-sovereign: only the merchant (msg.sender) writes
its own slot, no governance involvement.

Single delegate per merchant; calling `setSigner(addr)` overwrites the previous
delegate. Calling `setSigner(address(0))` clears delegation.


```solidity
mapping(address merchant => address signer) public merchantSigner
```


### merchantReceiver
Per-merchant default payout address — UX hint only.

pay() does NOT read this. The payout destination is the `receiver`
field of the signed Order. This mapping exists so dashboards /
off-chain order builders can fetch a sane default when creating
a new order. Self-sovereign: only the merchant (msg.sender) writes
its own slot. Rotating this value never invalidates already-signed
orders — each order pays to whichever receiver was signed at
order-creation time.


```solidity
mapping(address merchant => address receiver) public merchantReceiver
```


### _locked

```solidity
uint256 private _locked = 1
```


## Functions
### onlyGov


```solidity
modifier onlyGov() ;
```

### nonReentrant

Reentrancy guard; depth-1 protection against ERC777/ERC20 callbacks
and against native recipients that re-enter via receive().

Audit fix L-01: paired with CEI as defence-in-depth.


```solidity
modifier nonReentrant() ;
```

### constructor

Deploy the router.

No emergencyMultisig parameter — there is no emergency mode.
No per-tx fee cap — fee = amount * rate / 10000, linear.


```solidity
constructor(
    address _governance,
    address[] memory _initialTokens,
    address[] memory _initialRecipients,
    uint256 _initialFeeRate
) EIP712("BeamPayRouter", "1");
```
**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`_governance`|`address`|        Governance multisig (2/3 recommended).|
|`_initialTokens`|`address[]`|     Initial whitelist; include NATIVE_TOKEN to enable native pay.|
|`_initialRecipients`|`address[]`| Fee recipient pool (>= 1, recommended >= 3 per chain).|
|`_initialFeeRate`|`uint256`|    Starting fee rate in bps; must be <= FEE_RATE_HARD_LIMIT.|


### pay

Pay a merchant-signed order. Funds flow: payer -> receiver + payer -> feeRecipient,
all inside this single call.

Security notes:
- No whenNotPaused modifier; the contract has no pause state.
- CEI: state write (orders[key] = ...) happens before any external call.
- nonReentrant: defends against malicious token callbacks and native recipients.
- SafeERC20: tolerates non-standard ERC20s (e.g. mainnet USDT).
- try-fee leg: blacklisted fee recipient does not abort the receiver leg.
- amount > fee: prevents receiver from receiving 0 due to rounding.
- Native path: msg.value == amount required; ERC20 path: msg.value == 0 required.
- EIP-712 signature binds (chainId, verifyingContract, merchant, receiver, signer,
token, amount, orderId, createdAt, expiresAt). Any tamper invalidates the signature.
- `merchantReceiver[merchant]` is intentionally not consulted: `receiver` is the
single source of truth, fixed at signing time. Merchants may rotate the hint
without invalidating already-signed orders.


```solidity
function pay(
    address merchant,
    address receiver,
    address token,
    uint256 amount,
    bytes32 orderId,
    address signer,
    uint64 createdAt,
    uint64 expiresAt,
    bytes calldata signature
) external payable nonReentrant;
```
**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`merchant`|`address`|  Order owner (event index, refund caller); NOT the payout destination in v1.4+.|
|`receiver`|`address`|  Payout destination signed into the order (v1.4+). Must be non-zero.|
|`token`|`address`|     Token to pay with — must be whitelisted. Use NATIVE_TOKEN for native asset.|
|`amount`|`uint256`|    Order amount (pre-fee).|
|`orderId`|`bytes32`|   Merchant-scoped order id; must be unique per merchant.|
|`signer`|`address`|    Declared signer of the EIP-712 payload (must equal `merchant` or `merchantSigner[merchant]`).|
|`createdAt`|`uint64`| Timestamp (unix seconds) when the merchant signed the order.|
|`expiresAt`|`uint64`| Timestamp (unix seconds) after which the signature is no longer valid.|
|`signature`|`bytes`| EIP-712 secp256k1 signature over the `Order` struct (see ORDER_TYPEHASH).|


### refund

Refund (part of) an order. Callable only by the original merchant.

Security notes:
- msg.sender is implicitly the merchant (encoded into the order key). Only the
merchant can refund — the order's `receiver` has no refund authority.
- Token is read from the stored OrderRecord; merchant cannot specify a different
token. (v1.3: dropped the redundant `token` parameter that audit fix M-07
previously cross-checked — the stored value is the single source of truth.)
- Audit fix H-03: payer is forced from OrderRecord; not accepted as a parameter.
- Protocol fee is never refunded (on-chain cost is irreversible; merchant absorbs).
- Merchant must approve `amount` to this contract before calling (ERC20 path),
or attach msg.value == amount (native path).
- v1.4: the order's `receiver` slot is intentionally ignored here. Refunds
always flow to the original payer, regardless of who got paid out by pay().


```solidity
function refund(bytes32 orderId, uint256 amount) external payable nonReentrant;
```
**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`orderId`|`bytes32`|Merchant-side order id.|
|`amount`|`uint256`| Refund amount (partial refunds allowed; cumulative cap = order amount).|


### proposeFeeChange

Propose a new fee rate. Takes effect after TIMELOCK_DELAY.


```solidity
function proposeFeeChange(uint256 newRate) external onlyGov;
```
**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`newRate`|`uint256`|New rate in bps. Must be <= FEE_RATE_HARD_LIMIT.|


### executeFeeChange

Execute an already-matured fee change. Permissionless on purpose —
governance cannot delay approved proposals indefinitely.


```solidity
function executeFeeChange() external;
```

### cancelPendingChange

Governance-only: cancel a pending fee proposal.


```solidity
function cancelPendingChange() external onlyGov;
```

### addToken

Whitelist a token (or the native asset) so it can be used in pay().

Add-only: there is intentionally no removeToken/disableToken (see note above).
Pass NATIVE_TOKEN to enable native (ETH/BNB) payments. Reverts on zero address
or a token that is already allowed.


```solidity
function addToken(address token) external onlyGov;
```
**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`token`|`address`|Token address to allow, or NATIVE_TOKEN for the chain's native asset.|


### addFeeRecipient

Add a new fee recipient.

Audit fix H-04: zero-address + duplicate + size-cap guards.


```solidity
function addFeeRecipient(address recipient) external onlyGov;
```

### removeFeeRecipient

Remove a fee recipient.

Audit fix M-06: dual-parameter confirmation prevents accidental wrong removal.


```solidity
function removeFeeRecipient(uint256 index, address expectedAddress) external onlyGov;
```
**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`index`|`uint256`|Array index of the recipient to remove.|
|`expectedAddress`|`address`|Expected address at that index (guards against index drift between governance sign-off and on-chain execution).|


### transferGovernance

Begin a two-step governance handoff.

Audit fix M-03: the new governance must call acceptGovernance() to take effect,
preventing accidental permanent loss of access via a typo.


```solidity
function transferGovernance(address newGov) external onlyGov;
```

### acceptGovernance

New governance accepts the role.


```solidity
function acceptGovernance() external;
```

### renounceGovernance

Permanently renounce governance (irreversible).

After this call, governance == address(0). Every onlyGov function reverts.
Fee rate / whitelist / recipients freeze at their current values forever.
This is the "graduation" switch: once the protocol is stable, governance
can step away entirely.


```solidity
function renounceGovernance() external onlyGov;
```

### setSigner

Set or clear the merchant's authorized signing delegate.

msg.sender is the merchant. Passing `newSigner == address(0)` clears
delegation so only the merchant itself can sign subsequent orders.


```solidity
function setSigner(address newSigner) external;
```
**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`newSigner`|`address`|New delegate address (or address(0) to clear).|


### setReceiver

Set or clear the merchant's default payout receiver (UX hint only).

msg.sender is the merchant. `newReceiver == address(0)` clears the hint.
This is NOT validated against future order signatures — pay() uses the
receiver embedded in the signed order, never this mapping.


```solidity
function setReceiver(address newReceiver) external;
```
**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`newReceiver`|`address`|New default receiver (or address(0) to clear).|


### getFeeRecipients

Return all fee recipients (for front-end integrations).


```solidity
function getFeeRecipients() external view returns (address[] memory);
```

### feeRecipientsLength

Return the size of the fee-recipient pool.


```solidity
function feeRecipientsLength() external view returns (uint256);
```

### getOrder

Look up an order record. Returns a zero-valued struct (payer == address(0))
if no order was paid for this (merchant, orderId) pair.


```solidity
function getOrder(address merchant, bytes32 orderId) external view returns (OrderRecord memory);
```

### DOMAIN_SEPARATOR

EIP-712 domain separator for the current chain / contract address.

Front-ends should use this to verify they are signing against the correct domain.


```solidity
function DOMAIN_SEPARATOR() external view returns (bytes32);
```

### calculateFee

Compute the fee for a given amount under the current rate (linear, no cap).


```solidity
function calculateFee(uint256 amount) external view returns (uint256);
```

## Events
### Paid
Successful payment.


```solidity
event Paid(
    address indexed merchant,
    bytes32 indexed orderId,
    address indexed payer,
    address receiver,
    address token,
    uint256 amount,
    uint256 fee,
    address feeRecipient,
    bool feeCollected,
    uint256 timestamp
);
```

**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`merchant`|`address`|        Order owner (event-indexed; NOT the payout destination in v1.4+)|
|`orderId`|`bytes32`|         Merchant-side order id|
|`payer`|`address`|           Address that paid|
|`receiver`|`address`|        Payout destination signed into the order (v1.4+)|
|`token`|`address`|           Token used (NATIVE_TOKEN for native)|
|`amount`|`uint256`|          Pre-fee order amount|
|`fee`|`uint256`|             Protocol fee actually collected (= 0 on rate-zero or fail path)|
|`feeRecipient`|`address`|    Address fee was directed to (address(0) if no fee leg)|
|`feeCollected`|`bool`|    True if fee went to feeRecipient; false if redirected to receiver|
|`timestamp`|`uint256`|       Block timestamp of the payment|

### Refunded

```solidity
event Refunded(
    bytes32 indexed orderId,
    address indexed merchant,
    address indexed payer,
    address token,
    uint256 amount,
    uint256 timestamp
);
```

### FeeTransferFailed

```solidity
event FeeTransferFailed(bytes32 indexed orderId, address token, uint256 fee, address feeRecipient);
```

### FeeRedirectedToMerchant
Fee recipient was unreachable (blacklist, contract revert, etc.); fee was
redirected to the order's `receiver` to preserve the H-06 invariant.

Event name kept as `FeeRedirectedToMerchant` for indexer backward compatibility.
In v1.4+ the redirected fee lands at `receiver` (the payout destination), not
at the merchant — `merchant` is still emitted (indexed) as the order owner.


```solidity
event FeeRedirectedToMerchant(
    bytes32 indexed orderId,
    address token,
    uint256 fee,
    address indexed feeRecipient,
    address indexed merchant,
    address receiver
);
```

```solidity
event FeeChangeProposed(uint256 newRate, uint256 effectiveTime);
```

```solidity
event FeeChangeCancelled();
```

### SignerUpdated
Merchant updated its authorized signer delegate.


```solidity
event SignerUpdated(address indexed merchant, address indexed oldSigner, address indexed newSigner);
```

**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`merchant`|`address`| Merchant whose delegate slot was written.|
|`oldSigner`|`address`|Previous delegate (address(0) if none).|
|`newSigner`|`address`|New delegate (address(0) clears delegation).|

### ReceiverUpdated
Merchant updated its default payout-receiver hint (v1.4+).


```solidity
event ReceiverUpdated(address indexed merchant, address indexed oldReceiver, address indexed newReceiver);
```

**Parameters**

|Name|Type|Description|
|----|----|-----------|
|`merchant`|`address`|   Merchant whose receiver slot was written.|
|`oldReceiver`|`address`|Previous default receiver (address(0) if none).|
|`newReceiver`|`address`|New default receiver (address(0) clears the hint).|

### IncorrectNativeValue
msg.value did not match the native amount.


```solidity

```

### UnexpectedNativeValue
msg.value was sent on an ERC20 path.


```solidity
error UnexpectedNativeValue();
```

### NativeTransferFailed
Low-level .call{value:} to a recipient failed (out of gas, revert, etc.).


```solidity
error NativeTransferFailed();
```

### OrderExpired
block.timestamp > expiresAt: signed order is past its expiry window.


```solidity
error OrderExpired();
```

### UnauthorizedSigner
signer parameter is not the merchant and not the merchant's authorized delegate.


```solidity
error UnauthorizedSigner();
```

### InvalidSignature
ECDSA.recover(digest, signature) did not equal the declared `signer`.


```solidity
error InvalidSignature();
```

### InvalidExpiry
expiresAt <= createdAt: signed window is empty or inverted.


```solidity
error InvalidExpiry();
```

## Structs
### PendingChange
`effectiveTime == 0` is the sentinel for "no pending proposal".
A real proposal always sets `block.timestamp + TIMELOCK_DELAY`, which can
never be 0 on a live chain, so the sentinel is safe and we save the SSTORE
that a separate `bool exists` would have required (bool packs into its own
slot after a uint256, so removing it removes a full storage slot).


```solidity
struct PendingChange {
    uint256 newRate;
    uint256 effectiveTime;
}
```

### OrderRecord
Full order record stored per (merchant, orderId).

Audit fix M-08: merged processed/orderAmount/refunded into a single struct
to cut storage cost.
v1.0: dropped `exists` — `payer != address(0)` serves as the sentinel for
"order paid" (pay() always writes a non-zero msg.sender). Added
`signer`, `createdAt`, `expiresAt` from the EIP-712 signed payload.
v1.4: added `receiver` (signed in EIP-712). Storage layout (6 slots):
slot0: payer(20) + createdAt(8)
slot1: token(20)
slot2: amount(32)
slot3: refunded(32)
slot4: receiver(20)
slot5: signer(20) + expiresAt(8)


```solidity
struct OrderRecord {
    address payer; // 20B — non-zero iff order paid (replaces `exists`)
    uint64 createdAt; // 8B — timestamp from signed payload (packs with payer)
    address token; // 20B — token used for the order (refunds use same)
    uint256 amount; // 32B — original order amount (refund ceiling)
    uint256 refunded; // 32B — cumulative refunded amount
    address receiver; // 20B — payout destination from signed payload (v1.4+)
    address signer; // 20B — recovered EIP-712 signer (merchant or delegate)
    uint64 expiresAt; // 8B — timestamp from signed payload (packs with signer)
}
```
